## Contributing Guidelines

#### Thank you for your interest in contributing to this project. This document defines standards, workflows, and expectations for all contributors. It is designed to be reusable across different repositories.

---

### 1. Scope and Purpose

#### Contributions should align with the project's goals. Ensure that your work:

- Solves a real problem or improves functionality
- Maintains consistency and quality
- Does not introduce unnecessary complexity

---

### 2. Types of Contributions

#### We welcome:

- Bug fixes
- Feature development
- Performance improvements
- Refactoring
- Documentation updates
- Security enhancements
- Test improvements
- Pull Requests (code contributions)
- Issues (bug reports, feature requests, discussions)

---

### 3. Project Structure (Recommended)

- "core/" → main logic
- "modules/" → feature modules
- "utils/" → helper functions
- "config/" → configuration
- "docs/" → documentation
- "tests/" → testing

---

### 4. Contribution Workflow

1. Fork the repository
1. Clone your fork:

```bash
    git clone https://github.com/MatrixTM26/RAVEN-C2-Framework.git
    cd RAVEN-C2-Framework
```

1. Create a branch:

```bash
   git checkout -b feature/<feature-name>
```

1. Make changes
1. Commit:

```bash
   git add .
   git commit -m "feat: add <feature>"
```

1. Push:

```bash
   git push origin feature/<feature-name>
```

1. Open a Pull Request

---

5. Branch Naming

- "feature/`name`"
- "fix/`name`"
- "hotfix/`name`"
- "refactor/`name`"
- "docs/`name`"

---

### 6. Commit Standard

#### Format:

`type`: `description`

Types:

- feat
- fix
- docs
- refactor
- perf
- test
- chore

---

### 7. Coding Standards

- Follow best practices
- Use consistent formatting
- Use clear naming
- Avoid duplicated code
- Do not include sensitive data
- Keep code modular

---

### 8. Security Guidelines

- Validate inputs
- Avoid unsafe patterns
- Do not introduce vulnerabilities
- Report security issues responsibly

---

### 9. Testing

- Ensure no breaking changes
- Test new features
- Add tests if possible

---

### 10. Documentation

- Update documentation when needed
- Keep README consistent
- Provide examples

---

### 11. Issue Guidelines

#### When to Open an Issue

- Bug found
- Feature request
- Improvement discussion

#### Bug Template

[BUG]
Description:
Steps:
Expected:
Actual:
Environment:

#### Feature Template

[FEATURE]
Description:
Use case:
Benefits:

---

### 12. Pull Request Guidelines

- Keep PR small and focused
- One change per PR
- No unrelated changes

#### Include:

- Summary
- Reason
- Testing details
- Related issue (if any)

---

### 13. Code Review

- All PRs are reviewed
- Changes may be requested
- Approval required before merge

---

### 14. Responsible Use

#### Allowed:

- Education
- Research
- Authorized use

#### Prohibited:

- Illegal activity
- Unauthorized access
- Malicious usage

---

### 15. License

#### By contributing, you agree your work follows the project license.

---

### 16. Contact Me

- [GitHub](https://github.com/MatrixTM26)
- [Email](mailto:anonymous999tm@gmail.com)
- [Instagram](https://instagram.com/matrixtm26.xp)

---

> [!IMPORTANT]
> By contributing, you agree to follow all guidelines in this document.
